Joint Motion For Approval Of Paga Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Feb-18-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTs MIGUEL RODRIGUEZ and MIKEY'S TRANSPORT, LLC's Joint Motion For Approval Of Paga Settlement

The parties' renewed joint motion for approval of PAGA settlement is granted. Based on the information provided in plaintiff's supplemental briefing and supporting declaration, the Court finds that the overall amount of the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code Sec. 2699(l)(2); see Haralson v. U.S. Aviation Services Corp. (N.D. Cal. 2019) 383 F.Supp.3d 959, 972; O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2016) 201 F.Supp.3d 1110, 1132-35.)

Order is signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)

Joint Motion For Approval Of Paga Settlement; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Jan-26-2021 Continued To Feb-18-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES (VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE):
Atty. ANDREW E. HILLIER, Esq. of Hillier Law (Tel: 619-500-7906) for Plaintiff John Magana;
No other appearances made in this matter.

HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS MIGUEL RODRIGUEZ AND MIKEY'S TRANSPORT, LLC'z Joint Motion For Approval Of PAGA Settlement.

The Court's tentative ruling instructed parties to appear to address the schedule for submission of supplemental papers and a continued hearing.

Matter discussed.

The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The parties' joint motion for approval of PAGA settlement is denied without prejudice. The moving papers do not provide the Court with sufficient evidence to determine whether the overall amount of the proposed settlement is fair and adequate in view of the purposes and policies of PAGA, taking into account such factors as the adequacy of the civil penalties in light of the potential maximum if the matter were to go to verdict and the risks of litigation. (Lab. Code Sec. 2699(l)(2); see Haralson v. U.S. Aviation Services Corp. (N.D. Cal. 2019) 383 F.Supp.3d 959, 972; O'Connor v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (N.D. Cal. 2016) 201 F.Supp.3d 1110, 1132-35.) In particular, the parties do not establish the number of aggrieved employees who would benefit from the proposed settlement agreement. The memorandum asserts, citing to counsel's declaration, that "there are less than ten employees, including Plaintiff, who were paid in the same manner as Plaintiff," but counsel's declaration states that Defendant has "less than thirty" current employees. (Memo. at 3:25-27; Hillier Decl. par. 9(b).) Likewise, the moving papers do not establish the value of the PAGA claim, including the maximum amount of recoverable penalties, or provide any meaningful discussion of the merits of Plaintiff's claims or Defendant's defenses, or other reasons for discounting such an amount. In particular, they do not explain the basis for counsel's assertion that Plaintiff's individual claims "represent the vast majority of the settlement value of the case"; they do not explain counsel's contention that the "relatively small size of the Defendant entities . . . would likely result in a discretionary award of PAGA penalties less than the applicable statutory maximum, if they were awarded at all"; they do not describe what "compelling evidence" Defendants supposedly presented "to vitiate the value of the PAGA action"; and they do not provide any documentation supporting the contention that Plaintiff was "forced to take a significant discount on the settlement of his claims" in light of defendant's poor financial condition.

Parties to file their supplemental papers by February 11, 2021.

Matter continued for further hearing to February 18, 2021 at 9:30 a.m. in Dept. 320.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Sean Kane; Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR No. 8600, Email: maria.torreano@gmail.com (302/EPS)
